title: Carbon Footprint
definition: The total greenhouse gas emissions associated with a product, service, activity, or system.
whyItMatters: Design decisions affect material use, energy consumption, distribution, and end-of-life impacts.

title: Circular Economy
definition: An approach that keeps materials and products in use longer through reuse, repair, remanufacture, and recycling.
whyItMatters: It helps reduce waste and the extraction of virgin materials.

title: Emissions
definition: Greenhouse gases released into the atmosphere through energy use, manufacturing, transportation, or other activities.
whyItMatters: Reducing emissions is central to climate-responsive design and sustainable production systems.

title: Sustainable Materials
definition: Materials chosen to reduce environmental harm through renewability, recyclability, or lower lifecycle impact.
whyItMatters: Material choice often dominates environmental impact in physical design outputs.
